creative_brief: Clean, editorial layout with a muted earth-tone palette. Hero image: overhead shot of seasonal produce in reusable crates at the Brookvale hub. Below the fold, use simple supply-chain diagrams (farm → hub → you) alongside real photography of NSW farmland. No stock imagery. Typography should feel like a serious publication, not a startup pitch deck.
headline: You know the food system is broken. Now you can own the fix.
subhead: FarmThru is building a farm-direct grocery store that cuts out unnecessary middlemen. We're opening ownership to everyday people through equity crowdfunding — starting at $50.
hero_copy: You've watched prices climb while quality drops. You've wondered why an apple travels 2,400km to reach a shelf 30km from where it grew. You're not alone. FarmThru connects customers directly to partner farms across NSW through a hub-and-collect model — no unnecessary middlemen inflating the cost. And right now, you can secure your place as an owner. A $5 refundable deposit gets you VIP access before our campaign goes live.

sections.heading: The investment thesis in plain language
sections.body: Australians spend $130B a year on groceries. Yet most of that money feeds a supply chain designed for shareholders, not families or farmers. FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store. Customers order online and collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. No subscriptions. No lock-in. No membership fees. Just real food from roughly 15–20 partner farms and suppliers across NSW, priced fairly because we've removed the layers that inflate cost. The model rewards bulk buying — customers save more when they commit to larger orders, which means better margins and stronger retention. We believe this is a business worth building. We're inviting you to believe it too.

sections.heading: What your $5 refundable deposit unlocks
sections.body: Join the VIP waitlist and you get first access to our equity crowdfunding campaign when it launches. You'll receive priority updates on our progress, exclusive Q&A sessions with the founding team, and the chance to invest before the general public. The minimum investment is $50. The maximum is $10,000 per investor. Your $5 deposit is fully refundable — no commitment, no pressure. This is about giving you time to decide with full information, not rushing you into anything. We'll show you exactly where we are, what we're building, and what the risks look like. Then you choose.

sections.heading: Why this model, why now
sections.body: The big chains have optimised grocery for their benefit, not yours. Long supply chains mean food travels further, costs more, and farmers earn less. Direct-to-consumer food is growing at 24% year-on-year because people are tired of that equation. FarmThru's hub-and-collect model keeps overhead low. No sprawling retail leases. No fleet of delivery vans. Customers collect from our Brookvale hub — or we offer delivery across Sydney, Central Coast, and Wollongong. Every dollar we don't spend on unnecessary infrastructure is a dollar that keeps prices honest. Be part of it. Own a piece of the grocery store that actually shows you where your food comes from.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign will be hosted on Birchal. This content is not financial advice. Investing in early-stage businesses carries significant risk, including the risk of losing your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. Returns are not guaranteed. You should consider whether this investment is appropriate for your circumstances and seek independent financial advice if needed. Any investment decision should be made on the basis of the offer document, which will be available on the Birchal platform when the campaign launches.
